card.intuition: The hook that allows you to perform side effects (data fetching, subscriptions, DOM mutations) in functional components, running AFTER the browser has painted the screen.
card.analogy: A 'Post-it' note on the back of a painting: the painter finishes the artwork (renders the UI), hangs it on the wall (paints the screen), and only THEN reads the note to see if they need to call the gallery or turn on the spotlights (run effects).
when_to_use: Updating document.title or manually interacting with a 3rd party chart library
gotchas: If you don't provide a dependency array, the effect runs on EVERY render, which can lead to infinite loops if you update state inside the effect
Use `useLayoutEffect` if you need the effect to run BEFORE the browser paints (to avoid visual flickering)

steps.label: Render
steps.annotation: Component function runs — effects NOT yet executed.

steps.label: Commit
steps.annotation: React commits DOM changes. Browser paints.

steps.label: Effect runs
steps.annotation: useEffect callback runs after paint — does not block browser.

steps.label: Deps check
steps.annotation: On re-render: React checks dependency array. Changed deps → cleanup + re-run.

steps.label: Cleanup
steps.annotation: Cleanup function (return () => ...) runs before re-run and on unmount.

exercise.prompt: Identify the return value of useEffect used for cleaning up resources.
hints: It 'cleans up' the mess when the component leaves
